control.title: Scope-Aware Build Gate â€” auto-scope build/test/clippy to touched crates and stop false-aborting on pre-existing out-of-scope failures
control.executiveSummary: The super-dev build-gate (`src/build-runner.ts` + `src/stages/implementation.ts`) runs workspace-wide cargo build/test/clippy and then hard-terminates Stage 9 after 3 FAILs. On monorepos with PRE-EXISTING failures in crates the current branch never touched (e.g. stockfan-server spec 54: `reports_test.rs`, `stockfan-compute` clippy lints), this false-aborts Phase 1 while the phase's OWN code is green, so Phases 2â€“6 never run. Fix: (1) auto-derive the cargo package set from `git diff --merge-base main --name-only`, (2) scope ALL three gate commands (build + test + clippy) to that set, and (3) add an in-scope failure classification so the retry loop treats "all failures are pre-existing/out-of-scope" as phase-GREEN and only terminates-early on genuine in-scope failures. Backward compatible: empty scope or non-Cargo/non-git repos behave byte-identically to today.

acceptanceCriteria.statement: Add `detectTouchedCargoPackages(cwd, baseRef?): string[]` that spawns `git -C <cwd> diff --merge-base <baseRef> --name-only` (baseRef defaults to `"main"`, overridable via env `SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF`), maps every stdout line matching `crates/<pkg>/â€¦` to `<pkg>`, dedupes preserving first-seen order (reuse the existing `dedupePreservingOrder` helper), and returns `[]` on ANY git error, empty diff, or a diff containing only non-crate paths. The helper MUST NEVER throw (try/catch the spawnSync). `runBuildGate` auto-derives the scoped set from touched crates when the caller passes no packages and `SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES` is unset.

Generalize `scopedCargoTestArgs` into a shared `scopedCargoArgs(subcommand: "build"|"test"|"clippy", packages: string[], extraArgs?: string[]): string[]` emitting `["cargo", subcommand, ...packages.flatMap(p => ["-p", p]), ...(extraArgs ?? [])]`. Keep `scopedCargoTestArgs(packages)` as a thin wrapper returning `scopedCargoArgs("test", packages, ["--quiet"])` (existing call sites in verify.ts/implementation.ts + any tests unchanged), and add `scopedCargoBuildArgs(packages)` (`["cargo","build",â€¦,"--quiet"]` unscoped form) and `scopedCargoClippyArgs(packages)` (keeps `--all-targets --quiet`). Empty `packages` â†’ byte-identical workspace-wide argv for all three (`["cargo","build","--quiet"]`, `["cargo","test","--quiet"]`, `["cargo","clippy","--all-targets","--quiet"]`).

In `runBuildGate`, when `cmds0.language === "rust"` AND the resolved package set is non-empty, override `build`, `test`, AND `typecheck` on the shallow-copy `cmds` object (today only `test` is overridden). When the set is empty, all three stay workspace-wide and the `cmds` object is byte-identical to current behavior. Package-set precedence (highestâ†’lowest): explicit `opts.testPackages`/`opts.packages` (incl. explicit `[]` = force workspace-wide) â†’ `process.env.SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES` â†’ `detectTouchedCargoPackages(cwd)` â†’ workspace-wide. Detecting touched crates must not run when a higher-precedence source is present.

Extend `BuildGateResult` with `outOfScopeErrors: string[]` and `inScopePass: boolean`. After collecting `errors`, classify each error block: extract every source path it references via the `crates/<pkg>/` segment inside `--> <path>` markers AND `-p <pkg>` markers in cargo test-failure lines; an error is OUT-OF-SCOPE iff EVERY referenced crate is NOT in the resolved scoped set, otherwise IN-SCOPE. Compute `inScopePass = !gate.pass && gate.errors.length > 0 && gate.outOfScopeErrors.length === gate.errors.length` (all failures pre-existing/out-of-scope). When `gate.pass` is true, `inScopePass` is true and classification is a no-op. The classifier must NEVER throw; on any ambiguity it treats the error as IN-SCOPE (conservative â€” does not grant false green).

In `implementation.ts` retry loop, a phase is GREEN when `gate.pass || gate.inScopePass`. When green via `inScopePass`, log: `Implementation ${phaseId} IN-SCOPE GREEN on attempt ${attempt} â€” ${gate.outOfScopeErrors.length} pre-existing out-of-scope failure(s) ignored (crates: <comma-list>)`. The `if (!green) { â€¦ "failed after N attempts â€” terminating early"; allGreen=false; break; }` path fires ONLY when NEITHER pass nor inScopePass holds after MAX_ATTEMPTS (genuine in-scope failures). `runBuildGate(setup.worktreePath, { signal: ctx.signal })` call signature unchanged â€” scoping is derived internally.

Backward-compatibility / non-regression: non-Cargo repos, non-git dirs, no touched crates, and unset env vars produce IDENTICAL gate argvs and an IDENTICAL `runBuildGate` result to today (modulo the two additive fields), and the new in-scope classification never blocks when there are zero failures or no scoping is active (workspace-wide â‡’ every failure counts as in-scope â‡’ `inScopePass` stays false, preserving current abort semantics). `detectProjectCommands` purity is preserved (scoping applied only on the shallow copy).

New co-located unit/integration tests in `src/build-runner.test.ts` (and/or an `implementation` test) covering: (a) `detectTouchedCargoPackages` pathâ†’pkg mapping, dedupe, order preservation, git-failureâ†’`[]`, non-crate-only-diffâ†’`[]`, base-ref override via env; (b) `scopedCargoBuildArgs`/`scopedCargoTestArgs`/`scopedCargoClippyArgs` argv construction including empty-setâ†’byte-identical workspace-wide; (c) in-scope classification with scoped set `{data}` where a failure in `crates/data/â€¦` is IN-SCOPE and a failure in `crates/compute/â€¦` is OUT-OF-SCOPE, plus `inScopePass` true only when ALL failures are out-of-scope; (d) a `runBuildGate` integration that stubs `spawnSync`/git-diff so a touched-`data` crate drives all three commands to carry `-p data`. `npm run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit, strict) and `npm test` (vitest run) â€” existing AND new â€” all pass. No new runtime dependencies; no new spawned processes beyond existing gate commands plus one `git diff --name-only`.

nonFunctional: Performance / cost: Auto-scoping adds at most ONE additional spawnSync per gate run (`git -C <cwd> diff --merge-base <baseRef> --name-only`) and is skipped entirely when a higher-precedence package source is present; on large monorepos it REDUCES wall-time by scoping the three cargo commands. The git-diff helper must use the existing spawnSync pattern with the resolved timeout envelope, never an unbounded shell.

Robustness / safety: `detectTouchedCargoPackages` and the in-scope classifier MUST NEVER throw â€” any git error, malformed output, or regex ambiguity degrades to workspace-wide behavior / conservative in-scope classification. This preserves the 'never false-abort on green, never false-pass on broken' contract.

Backward-compatibility / determinism: empty resolved scope â‡’ byte-identical argvs and result to today; the two new `BuildGateResult` fields are additive and no-op when there are no failures or no active scoping. `detectProjectCommands` stays pure (overrides only on the shallow copy).

Constraint isolation: pure TS change to `src/build-runner.ts`, `src/stages/implementation.ts`, and tests only â€” nodes.ts / workflow.ts / pipeline.ts / render templates / control-flow engine untouched. Target repo never mutated by the gate.
No new runtime dependencies; no new spawned processes beyond existing gate commands + one `git diff --name-only`.
openQuestions: When the resolved scoped set is EMPTY (workspace-wide), should `inScopePass` be forced false so the new field can NEVER grant a green the old code wouldn't? RECOMMENDED: yes â€” treat workspace-wide as 'every failure is in-scope', so `inScopePass` is only ever true when active cargo scoping produced an all-out-of-scope failure set. This keeps non-Cargo and non-scoped runs byte-identical.

`detectTouchedCargoPackages` reads `git diff --merge-base main` which reflects COMMITTED changes in the worktree. If the implementer created a brand-new crate not yet committed, it won't be scoped. RECOMMENDED: accept this and rely on the per-phase commit step having run before the gate (current implementation.ts commits AFTER green, so the FIRST attempt may run before the new crate is committed) â€” note as a known edge case; fallback in-scope classification still protects against false-abort.

Base-ref default is literally `"main"`. Some repos default to `master`/`develop`. RECOMMENDED: ship `"main"` as the literal default per spec (env `SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF` covers the rest) and note default-branch auto-detection as future work; if `main` doesn't exist, `git diff --merge-base` fails and the helper returns `[]` (safe workspace-wide fallback).

Should the in-scope classification also be surfaced into the log/stderr tail consumed by the implementer's next attempt (so it does NOT retry against pre-existing failures)? RECOMMENDED: yes â€” when `inScopePass` is the reason for green, the retry loop does not run another attempt, so no fix-loop waste; when genuine in-scope failures remain, feed only the IN-SCOPE subset into `attemptErrors` so the implementer fixes real failures, not pre-existing noise.

control.title: Scope-aware build-gate: touched-set scoping + in-scope failure classification (research brief)
control.summary: The build-gate false-aborts because it runs `cargo build && cargo test && cargo clippy --all-targets` workspace-wide and treats any failure â€” including pre-existing ones in untouched crates â€” as a strike toward the 3-attempt terminate-early. The industry-standard fix is a *touched-set* scoping strategy: detect files changed against the merge-base of `main`, map `crates/<pkg>/â€¦` paths to crate names, and re-run `cargo build/test/clippy` with `-p <pkg>` per touched crate (cargo's documented package-selection flag [SRC-001]; `git diff --merge-base <base>` diffs against the common ancestor so diverged/rebased branches work correctly [SRC-002]). Because a workspace may still carry pre-existing failures that the touched-set cannot subtract, the gate additionally needs a conservative *in-scope* failure classifier: only grant a green verdict (`inScopePass`) when EVERY observed failure line maps (via `crates/<pkg>/` and `-p <pkg>` markers) to a crate NOT in the touched set, and on any ambiguity default to IN-SCOPE so the hard oracle never hands out a false pass. The gate/git helpers must be total (never throw â€” degrade to workspace-wide). The baseline-diff alternative (run the gate on `main` and subtract failure sets) is more robust but 2Ã— the gate cost and was correctly deferred to future work.

options.name: Touched-set scoping (chosen): git diff --merge-base main --name-only â†’ map crates/<pkg>/ â†’ -p flags on build+test+clippy
options.tradeoffs: Cheapest correct option â€” one extra `git diff --name-only` (no new process class), reuses existing spawnSync, byte-identical backward compat when the set is empty. Grounded by cargo book (-p selects packages [SRC-001]) and git docs (--merge-base [SRC-002]). Tradeoffs: cannot tell a failure that is genuinely pre-existing INSIDE a touched crate (that needs baseline-diff #3), and assumes dir-name == crate-name and a `crates/<pkg>/` layout â€” repos with other layouts (libs/, root crate, or member-nameâ‰ dir) degrade to workspace-wide. Conservative classifier masks that last gap because ambiguous = IN-SCOPE.

options.name: Baseline-diff / known-good-baseline (#3, deferred): run the full gate on the base ref and subtract its failure set
options.tradeoffs: Most accurate â€” catches 'my crate was already broken on main' and is layout/name-agnostic. But ~2Ã— gate runtime (a second full build/test/clippy on main), needs failure-set persistence + flake handling (baseline failures can be non-deterministic), and the classifier still needs the marker parsing. Correctly out-of-scope for this pass; the conservative in-scope classifier (#4) covers the common pre-existing-out-of-scope case at far lower cost.

options.name: In-scope classifier posture: conservative (default IN-SCOPE on ambiguity) â€” chosen
options.tradeoffs: For a HARD test oracle the asymmetry favors false-fail over false-pass: a false pass lets a regression ship, a false fail just costs a retry. Defaulting ambiguous/unparseable failures to IN-SCOPE (and only setting inScopePass when ALL failures map to untouched crates) guarantees no false green. Lenient posture would hide real regressions behind truncated stderr tails or unfamiliar output formats.

options.name: Diff base selection: merge-base (chosen) vs direct two-dot main..HEAD / origin/main
options.tradeoffs: merge-base diffs against the common ancestor of HEAD and main, so a branch that rebased/merged main compares against the right point (git docs confirm `--merge-base` semantics [SRC-002]). Direct `main`/`origin/main` refs drift after a rebase and can show an empty diff (false workspace-wide) or a polluted diff. Overridable via SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF for repos whose default branch isn't `main`.

openIssues: ISS-001 (crate-name vs directory): The touched-set maps `crates/<pkg>/` to `-p <pkg>`, assuming the on-disk directory name equals the Cargo workspace member/package name. Cargo members can be named differently from their path (crate at `crates/foo/` published as `bar`). If names diverge, `cargo -p data` won't resolve. Robust fix is to read root Cargo.toml `[workspace].members` to map pathâ†’name, which is beyond this pass. Confirms safe for stockfan-server (convention holds); note as future hardening.

ISS-002 (layout assumption): Scoping only triggers for `crates/<pkg>/` paths. Other common layouts (`libs/`, `xtask/`, `bin/`, root-crate edits) produce no `crates/` match â†’ `[]` â†’ workspace-wide fallback. Safe (no false scope) but loses scoping benefit; a non-crates diff maps to `[]` per AC-01. Document as known limitation.

ISS-003 (git context): `git -C <cwd> diff --merge-base main` requires `main` to resolve in the worktree (shallow/disconnected clones, detached HEAD, or a repo whose default branch isn't `main` can fail). AC-01's try/catchâ†’`[]` degrades correctly; the SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF override covers non-`main` defaults. Add a test asserting a git error returns `[]` (already required by AC-07a).

ISS-004 (stderr tail truncation): The classifier parses `--> <path>` and `-p <pkg>` markers from only the last ~12 stderr lines per command (STDERR_TAIL_LINES). A failure whose source path falls outside the captured tail is unclassifiable â†’ treated IN-SCOPE (conservative), so it won't grant a false green but may still trigger an unnecessary retry/terminate. Increasing the tail or adopting baseline-diff (#3) is the long-term fix.

ISS-005 (clippy --all-targets under -p): When scoping clippy, `--all-targets --quiet` must be preserved (AC-02). Verify scoped form is `cargo clippy -p data --all-targets --quiet` and that `-p` before `--all-targets` does not change which targets are linted for that package (cargo treats `-p` as a workspace-member filter, `--all-targets` as a target-kind filter â€” orthogonal, so order-insensitive).

result.text: I have everything needed to resolve all 5 open issues. The Cargo book confirms `-p spec` selects by package **name** and is orthogonal to `--all-targets` (ISS-001, ISS-005); git docs confirm `--merge-base` semantics and failure modes (ISS-003); the code confirms `STDERR_TAIL_LINES=12` and the conservative classification path (ISS-004). All 5 issues resolve to accepted-design-decisions with safe degradation, not blockers.

control.title: Scope-aware build-gate: resolving crate-scoping + in-scope-classification open issues
control.summary: Follow-up research resolving the 5 open issues (ISS-001..ISS-005) blocking the scope-aware build-gate task. All five resolve to ACCEPTED design decisions with safe degradation â€” none block implementation, so openIssues is empty. ISS-001 (crate-name vs directory): Cargo's `-p spec`/`--package spec` selects by package NAME, not path (Cargo book, SRC-001), so a member at `crates/foo/` named `bar` makes `cargo -p foo` unresolved; this is safe for stockfan-server where dir==name by convention and is accepted as a known limitation this pass â€” the robust future fix is `cargo metadata --no-deps --format-version 1` (authoritative member name + manifest_path map), NOT raw `[workspace].members` parsing, because `[workspace].members` lists path globs, not package names. ISS-002 (layout): path-prefix scoping `crates/<pkg>/` means libs/xtask/bin/root-crate diffs yield `[]` â†’ workspace-wide fallback (AC-01); no false scope, just lost scoping benefit â€” documented limitation. ISS-003 (git context): `git diff --merge-base <commit>` is exactly `git diff $(git merge-base <commit> HEAD)` (SRC-002); an unresolved ref (shallow/disconnected clone, detached HEAD, unborn branch, non-main default) exits non-zero â†’ AC-01's try/catch degrades to `[]` â†’ workspace-wide, and SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF covers non-main defaults; AC-07a's git-errorâ†’[] test pins this. ISS-004 (stderr tail): STDERR_TAIL_LINES=12 (build-runner.ts:147); a failure whose `--> <path>`/`-p <pkg>` marker falls outside the captured tail is unclassifiable â†’ treated IN-SCOPE (conservative), so it never grants a false green but may cause an unnecessary retry/terminate â€” accepted; a cheap near-term mitigation is bumping the tail (12â†’~40), full fix is the deferred baseline-diff (#3). ISS-005 (clippy --all-targets): Cargo `-p` (package/workspace-member filter) and `--all-targets` (target-KIND filter = --lib --bins --tests --benches --examples, SRC-001) are orthogonal and order-insensitive, so `cargo clippy -p data --all-targets --quiet` lints every target kind of the `data` package while preserving the required `--all-targets --quiet` flags (AC-02) â€” confirmed correct. Net: the touched-set + conservative in-scope classifier design is sound and ready to implement; the in-scope verdict (gate.pass || gate.inScopePass) correctly stops the false-abort on pre-existing out-of-scope failures without ever risking a false green.

options.name: ISS-001 crate-name mapping: dir-name heuristic now, cargo metadata later (CHOSEN)
options.tradeoffs: This pass maps `crates/<pkg>/` -> `-p <pkg>` assuming dir==package-name, which holds for stockfan-server by convention. Verified that `-p` is name-based (SRC-001), so divergence makes `cargo -p <dir>` unresolved rather than silently wrong â€” i.e. a hard gate failure, not a false pass, so the failure mode is safe-strict. Pros: zero new deps, one git diff, byte-identical backward compat. Con: loses scoping (falls to workspace-wide) or hard-fails the gate if a repo's dir!=name, which re-triggers the very false-abort we're fixing. ACCEPTED for this pass; robust future fix is `spawnSync('cargo', ['metadata','--no-deps','--format-version','1'])` and parse members' `name`+`manifest_path` for an authoritative path->name map â€” avoid hand-parsing `[workspace].members` since it lists path globs, not names. Note in 10-implementation-summary.md as future hardening.

options.name: ISS-004 classifier posture: conservative default-IN-SCOPE with modest tail bump (CHOSEN)
options.tradeoffs: For a HARD test oracle the asymmetry favors false-fail over false-pass: a false pass ships a regression, a false fail only costs a retry. Defaulting ambiguous/unparseable failures (incl. those truncated outside STDERR_TAIL_LINES=12) to IN-SCOPE, and granting inScopePass only when ALL failures map to untouched crates, guarantees no false green. Pros: cheap, never dangerous. Con: may cause an unnecessary retry/terminate when a real (in-scope) failure's path marker is clipped by the 12-line tail. Mitigation: bump STDERR_TAIL_LINES to ~40 (cheap, still bounded) to widen classification coverage; full layout/format-independence is the deferred baseline-diff (#3). ACCEPTED.

options.name: ISS-003 base ref: git merge-base (default main, env-overridable) + total try/catch (CHOSEN)
options.tradeoffs: `git diff --merge-base <commit>` diffs against the common ancestor of <commit> and HEAD (SRC-002), so a branch that rebased/merged main compares at the right point â€” more correct than a bare two-dot `main..HEAD`, which drifts/empties after a rebase. An unresolved ref (shallow/disconnected/detached/unborn/non-main) exits non-zero; AC-01's try/catch->[] degrades to workspace-wide and SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF covers non-main defaults. Pros: rebase-safe, total (never throws), env-escapable. Con: needs `main` (or configured ref) present; covered by the git-error->[] test (AC-07a). ACCEPTED.

options.name: ISS-005 clippy scoping: preserve --all-targets --quiet, -p is orthogonal (CONFIRMED)
options.tradeoffs: Cargo docs (SRC-001) show `-p spec` filters which packages and `--all-targets` filters which target KINDS (lib+bins+tests+benches+examples); they are independent axes and order-insensitive. So `cargo clippy -p data --all-targets --quiet` lints every target kind of `data` exactly as the workspace-wide form lints every kind â€” no loss of coverage, no behavior change beyond scoping the package set. scopedCargoClippyArgs(packages) should emit `[

options.name: ISS-002 layout coverage: crates/ prefix with graceful [] fallback (CONFIRMED)
options.tradeoffs: Scoping triggers only for `crates/<pkg>/...` diffs; libs/xtask/bin/root-crate edits yield no match -> [] -> workspace-wide (AC-01). This is safe-by-design: no false scope is ever applied, so behavior is byte-identical to today for non-crates diffs, and the in-scope classifier then counts every failure as IN-SCOPE (no false green). Pro: simplest correct scoping that needs no manifest parsing. Con: loses scoping benefit on non-`crates/` layouts. ACCEPTED as documented limitation; a future layout-aware detector could consult `[workspace].members` globs, but that overlaps with the ISS-001 cargo-metadata hardening.

result.text: I now have full evidence. I've confirmed all the code anchors: `runBuildGate` overrides only `cmds.test` (build-runner.ts:301), `implementation.ts:64` passes no packages, and the `break` at :74-78 aborts all phases. No `detectTouchedCargoPackages`, no `scopedCargoBuildArgs`/`scopedCargoClippyArgs`, and no `inScopePass`/`outOfScopeErrors` fields exist. Existing tests live in `tests/build-runner-*.test.ts` and use the `result.ran` label-reading pattern with env save/restore. Producing the analysis.

control.title: RCA: scope-blind build-gate false-fails then false-aborts on pre-existing out-of-scope failures
control.summary: On the 2026-07-19 stockfan-server spec-54 run, the timeout fix worked and Phase 1's OWN code was demonstrably green (agents verified 273 stockfan-data + 10 stockfan-api tests via `cargo test -p â€¦`), yet Stage 9 implementation aborted after Phase 1 ("phase-01 failed after 3 attempts â€” terminating early"), so Phases 2â€“6 never ran. The build-gate reported FAIL Ã—3 on three pre-existing, out-of-scope failures: `reports_test.rs` (a different Reports-API feature), `stockfan-compute` clippy lints 18â€“30 (a different crate's tech debt), and `job_queries_test.rs`. Root cause is two compounding scope-blind defects. (A) `runBuildGate` (src/build-runner.ts:282) scopes ONLY `cmds0.test` at line 301 â€” `build` and `typecheck` (clippy) stay workspace-wide â€” and even that `test` scoping is gated on an explicit/env package list that was never set in this run, so all three commands ran workspace-wide `cargo build && cargo test && cargo clippy --all-targets`. (B) The implementation retry loop (src/stages/implementation.ts:43â€“78) has a binary 3-strikes-terminate: it retries up to MAX_ATTEMPTS=3 feeding `gate.errors` back to the implementer each round, but those errors are pre-existing/out-of-scope â€” the implementer cannot fix them â€” so all three attempts repeat-FAIL, then `if (!green) { â€¦ break; }` at :74â€“78 aborts ALL remaining phases. The gate cannot tell "my failures" from "the repo's pre-existing failures," so it false-aborts on green code. The fix set addresses both: (1) `detectTouchedCargoPackages(cwd, baseRef)` auto-derives the scoped crate set from `git diff --merge-base main --name-only`; (2) generalize `scopedCargoTestArgs` â†’ `scopedCargoArgs("build"|"test"|"clippy", â€¦)` so build+test+clippy ALL carry `-p` when the resolved set is non-empty (empty â†’ byte-identical workspace-wide); (3) add `outOfScopeErrors[]` + `inScopePass:boolean` to `BuildGateResult` (classify by `crates/<pkg>/` from `--> <path>` markers and `-p <pkg>` markers; conservative â†’ in-scope on ambiguity); (4) implementation.ts treats `gate.pass || gate.inScopePass` as phase-GREEN and only terminates-early on genuine in-scope failures. Backward-compatible: non-cargo, non-git, no-touched-crates, and unset-env paths produce identical results to today (the two additive fields never block when there are zero failures).
hypotheses: H1 (70%): The gate ran all three commands workspace-wide. No scoping source was active (no `SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES` env, implementation.ts:64 passes no `opts.testPackages`), AND even when scoping IS active commit 97fc4df6 only overrides `cmds.test` (build-runner.ts:301) leaving `build` and `clippy` workspace-wide. Prediction: out-of-scope crates' pre-existing failures (reports_test.rs, stockfan-compute clippy lints, job_queries_test.rs) flow into `gate.errors`; setting SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES=stockfan-data would still leave cargo build and cargo clippy workspace-wide and FAIL. Confirmed by code read: only `cmds.test` is overridden.

H2 (20%): Even with a touched-crate-scoped gate, the retry loop (implementation.ts:74â€“78) treats ANY non-pass as hard failure â†’ `break` after 3 attempts â†’ false-abort, because there is no in-scope/out-of-scope distinction. The implementer cannot fix pre-existing out-of-scope failures, so all 3 attempts repeat-FAIL identically. Prediction: a scoped gate that still leaks one out-of-scope failure (e.g. via a dependency compile/clippy ripple) still aborts the run. This is the second-order defect the in-scope classifier must defuse.

H3 (8%): A genuine in-scope regression the implementer could not fix in 3 attempts. Prediction: re-running Phase 1's own `cargo test -p stockfan-data -p stockfan-api` in isolation FAILS. FALSIFIED by the run log â€” agents verified 273+10 tests green via package-scoped cargo, so the branch's own code passed.

H4 (2%): Environmental flake (ENOENT, race, ETIMEDOUT). Prediction: failures are non-deterministic / tied to missing binaries. Largely falsified: the timeout fix produced no ETIMEDOUT, and the three failures are deterministic, named test files / clippy lints, not transient.
control.rootCause:
Two compounding scope-blindness defects, neither of which can distinguish the branch's own failures from the repo's pre-existing failures:

(1) OVER-SCOPING OF COMMANDS â€” src/build-runner.ts `runBuildGate` (line 282) builds the shallow-copy `cmds` by overriding ONLY `cmds0.test` at line 301 (`{ ...cmds0, test: scopedCargoTestArgs(testPackages) }`). `build` and `typecheck` (clippy) are never scoped. Worse, `testPackages` resolves ONLY from `opts.testPackages` or `process.env.SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES` (lines 291â€“294); there is no auto-derivation from what the branch actually touched. The implementation caller (src/stages/implementation.ts:64) invokes `runBuildGate(setup.worktreePath, { signal: ctx.signal })` with no packages and the env var was unset, so all three commands ran the blunt workspace-wide `cargo build && cargo test && cargo clippy --all-targets`, dragging in the pre-existing `reports_test.rs`, `stockfan-compute` clippy lints, and `job_queries_test.rs` failures that live in crates the branch never modified. Confirmed by grep: no `scopedCargoBuildArgs`/`scopedCargoClippyArgs`/`detectTouchedCargoPackages` exist; only `scopedCargoTestArgs` is defined (line 142) and called once (line 301).

(2) BINARY 3-STRIKES-TERMINATE â€” src/stages/implementation.ts retry loop (lines 43â€“78) sets `green=false`, loops `attempt 1..MAX_ATTEMPTS(=3)`, feeds the previous attempt's `gate.errors` back into the implementer prompt each round (line 54), and on each non-pass logs "attempt N/3 FAIL". Because the failures are pre-existing/out-of-scope, the implementer cannot remove them, so all three attempts FAIL identically. Then line 74 `if (!green) { â€¦ "failed after N attempts â€” terminating early"; allGreen=false; break; }` fires: the `break` exits the phase loop and aborts ALL remaining phases (2â€“6). There is no in-scope classifier and no `inScopePass` field on `BuildGateResult`, so the loop has no way to treat an all-out-of-scope failure as phase-green.

Composition: defect (1) injects out-of-scope failures into `gate.errors`; defect (2) escalates those into a hard abort. The fix mirrors this: (1) auto-scope build+test+clippy to touched crates via `detectTouchedCargoPackages` + a generalized `scopedCargoArgs`, and (2) add `outOfScopeErrors`/`inScopePass` classification so `gate.pass || gate.inScopePass` is treated as GREEN and only genuine in-scope failures terminate early.
reproductionSteps: Minimal code-level repro (confirms defect A + missing helpers): grep -n 'scopedCargoBuildArgs\|scopedCargoClippyArgs\|detectTouchedCargoPackages\|inScopePass\|outOfScopeErrors' src/build-runner.ts src/stages/implementation.ts â†’ all absent; confirm src/build-runner.ts:301 only overrides `cmds.test` and src/stages/implementation.ts:64 passes no packages. This alone proves build+clippy run workspace-wide and the retry loop is scope-blind.

Workspace repro (mirrors the stockfan run): in a Rust workspace monorepo (root Cargo.toml, crates under crates/), seed a PRE-EXISTING failing test in an untouched crate (crates/reports/reports_test.rs assert_eq!(1,2)) and pre-existing clippy lints in another untouched crate (crates/compute/). Branch from main touching ONLY crates/data/ (clean, green).

Run the super-dev implementation stage against a spec whose phase-1 maps to that branch's worktree with NO SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES and NO opts.testPackages. Inspect gate.ran: it records argv.join(' ') pushed before spawnSync, so you'll see 'cargo build --quiet', 'cargo test --quiet', 'cargo clippy --all-targets --quiet' â€” all workspace-wide, no -p flags.

Observe gate.pass === false because gate.errors contains the out-of-scope reports_test.rs failure + compute clippy lints (exit-nonzero tail). Then watch the retry loop: attempt 1 FAIL â†’ errors fed to attempt 2 â†’ attempt 2 FAIL (identical out-of-scope errors) â†’ attempt 3 FAIL â†’ log 'phase-01 failed after 3 attempts â€” terminating early' â†’ allGreen=false; break (Phases 2â€“N skipped).

Falsify the in-scope hypothesis (proves the abort was false): run `cargo test -p data` (the only touched crate) in the same worktree â†’ GREEN. The failures were entirely pre-existing/out-of-scope, confirming the run was false-aborted on green code.

Scoping-leak repro (confirms defect A even when env IS set): re-run step 3 with SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES=data. Inspect gate.ran: 'cargo test -p data --quiet' IS scoped (proving 97fc4df6 works for test), but 'cargo build --quiet' and 'cargo clippy --all-targets --quiet' remain workspace-wide â€” they still FAIL on the out-of-scope crates. This proves build+clippy must be scoped too (AC-02/AC-03), not just test.

Unit-test harness contract (from tests/build-runner-*.test.ts pattern, hermetic, no real cargo): new tests assert (a) detectTouchedCargoPackages maps 'crates/data/src/lib.rs'â†’['data'], dedupes preserving order, returns [] on git error / empty diff / non-crate-only diff, honors SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF; (b) scopedCargoBuildArgs([])/scopedCargoTestArgs([])/scopedCargoClippyArgs([]) are byte-identical workspace-wide argvs; (c) with scoped set {data}, a failure referencing crates/data/â€¦ is IN-SCOPE and one referencing crates/compute/â€¦ is OUT-OF-SCOPE, and inScopePass is true only when ALL failures are out-of-scope; (d) a runBuildGate integration that stubs spawnSync/git-diff so a touched 'data' crate drives build+test+clippy argvs to all carry '-p data'. npm run typecheck + npm test must stay green.

control.title: Codebase Assessment: Scope-Aware Build Gate (pi-super-dev)
control.summary: pi-super-dev is a TypeScript pi-extension (ESM, `"type":"module"`, strict tsconfig, NodeNext module resolution) â€” a 13-stage development pipeline orchestrator, NOT a running server. The target change is scoped to two files: `src/build-runner.ts` (the deterministic build/test/typecheck gate, a spawnSync-based side-effecting module) and `src/stages/implementation.ts` (Stage 9 per-phase TDD loop that consumes the gate). The codebase has an exceptionally well-established pattern to follow: commit 97fc4df6 already added timeout + test-scoping via a clean precedence chain (explicit opts â†’ env var â†’ default) implemented as PURE, fully-documented, unit-testable helpers (`resolveTimeoutMs`, `parseTestPackages`, `scopedCargoTestArgs`) plus a SHALLOW-COPY scoping step in `runBuildGate` that keeps `detectProjectCommands` byte-identical. The required work is a faithful generalization of that exact pattern: (1) add `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (one `git diff --name-only`, never throws), (2) generalize `scopedCargoTestArgs`â†’`scopedCargoArgs(subcommand,...)` + build/clippy wrappers, (3) scope all three rust argvs (build/test/typecheck) instead of only `:301`'s test, (4) extend `BuildGateResult` with `outOfScopeErrors`+`inScopePass` and make implementation.ts treat `inScopePass` as GREEN. Tests are vitest, test-first (RED-phase), AC/SCENARIO-tagged describe blocks, with `tmpProj`/`withEnv` helpers and assertions reading `result.ran` (argv joined pre-spawn). Verification is `npm run typecheck` + `npm test` â€” there is NO API/UI server to start (services omitted). Backward compatibility is paramount: every fallback (non-rust, non-git, no touched crates, unset env) must remain byte-identical to today.

control.title: Scope-Aware Build Gate â€” Auto-scope cargo gate to touched crates and classify in-scope vs pre-existing out-of-scope failures
control.summary:
TECHNICAL SPECIFICATION.

Problem: The super-dev build-gate (`src/build-runner.ts#runBuildGate`) runs `cargo build && cargo test && cargo clippy --all-targets` WORKSPACE-WIDE. On messy real-world Rust monorepos (evidence: failed run 2026-07-19 on stockfan-server spec 54), pre-existing failures in crates the current branch never touched (e.g. `reports_test.rs`, `stockfan-compute` clippy lints, `job_queries_test.rs`) flip the gate FAIL. The implementation retry loop (`src/stages/implementation.ts`) treats every gate FAIL identically, and after `MAX_ATTEMPTS=3` binary-terminates ALL remaining phases ("terminating early"). Net: Stage 9 aborts on green in-scope code, so Phases 2â€“6 never run. Two root causes: (a) commit 97fc4df6 only scopes the `test` argv (`build-runner.ts:301` overrides only `cmds.test`), leaving `build` and `clippy`/typecheck workspace-wide; (b) the retry loop has no concept of in-scope vs out-of-scope failures.

Scope: Pure TypeScript change to `src/build-runner.ts` + `src/stages/implementation.ts` (+ co-located tests). No changes to `nodes.ts`, `workflow.ts`, `pipeline.ts`, render templates, or the control-flow engine. No new runtime deps; no new spawned processes beyond existing gate commands plus one `git diff --merge-only`.

Solution (4 capabilities):
1. Auto-detect touched cargo crates via `git -C <cwd> diff --merge-base <baseRef> --name-only` (AC-01) and map `crates/<pkg>/â€¦` â†’ `<pkg>`, deduped, never throwing.
2. Scope ALL THREE gate commands (build + test + clippy) â€” not just test â€” by generalizing `scopedCargoTestArgs` into a shared `scopedCargoArgs(subcommand, packages, extraArgs?)` family (AC-02) and overriding `build`/`test`/`typecheck` on the shallow `cmds` copy (AC-03).
3. Classify gate failures as in-scope vs out-of-scope using `crates/<pkg>/` and `-p <pkg>` markers, adding `outOfScopeErrors: string[]` + `inScopePass: boolean` to `BuildGateResult` (AC-04).
4. Treat `gate.pass || gate.inScopePass` as phase-GREEN in the retry loop, logging ignored pre-existing failures, and terminate-early ONLY on genuine in-scope failures (AC-05).

Constraints/precedence: Package-set resolution precedence (highestâ†’lowest): explicit `opts.testPackages`/`opts.packages` (incl. explicit `[]` = force workspace-wide) â†’ `process.env.SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES` â†’ `detectTouchedCargoPackages(cwd)` â†’ workspace-wide. `SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF` overrides the base ref (default `"main"`). Git-diff is skipped when a higher-precedence source is present. The classifier is conservative: on any ambiguity an error is treated IN-SCOPE (never grants a false green). Backward-compatibility: non-Cargo repos, non-git dirs, no-touched-crates, and unset env vars produce IDENTICAL argvs and an IDENTICAL `runBuildGate` result to today (modulo two additive fields `outOfScopeErrors`/`inScopePass`, which never block when there are zero failures or no scoping is active).

All 7 ACs (AC-01â€¦AC-07) addressed; all 29 scenarios (SCENARIO-001â€¦SCENARIO-029) mapped. Acceptance: `npm run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit, strict) clean and `npm test` (vitest run) green for existing + new tests.

Out of scope (future work, to be noted in 10-implementation-summary.md): Full baseline-diff (#3 â€” running the gate on `main` and subtracting the failure set). The in-scope classification (#4) covers the common pre-existing-failure case more cheaply; baseline-diff remains a future enhancement for edge cases (e.g. failures with no parseable crate marker).
control.architecture:
ARCHITECTURE. Two files change; the module boundary (build-runner as the side-effecting deterministic oracle; implementation.ts as the per-phase retry driver) is preserved.

### Module: src/build-runner.ts

**(1) Touched-crate detection â€” AC-01.** Add `export function detectTouchedCargoPackages(cwd: string, baseRef?: string): string[]`. Contract: input `cwd` (absolute worktree path) and optional `baseRef` (defaults to `process.env.SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF` if set else `"main"`). Behaviour: wrap a `spawnSync("git", ["-C", cwd, "diff", "--merge-base", baseRef, "--name-only"], { encoding: "utf8" })` in `try { } catch { return []; }`. On `r.status !== 0`, `r.error`, empty stdout, or any thrown error â†’ `return []`. Otherwise parse stdout lines; for each line, match the FIRST `crates/<pkg>/` segment via regex `/(?:^|\/)crates\/([^/]+)\//` (or split on `/` and detect `crates` segment followed by the package name); map each matching line to `<pkg>`; lines with no `crates/` segment are ignored (non-crate paths, e.g. root `Cargo.toml`, `README`). Dedupe with the existing `dedupePreservingOrder` helper preserving first-seen order. MUST NEVER throw (the entire body is try/caught). Pure wrt argv construction but reads env + spawns git (side-effecting) â€” same category as the rest of the module.

**(2) Scoped argv family â€” AC-02.** Add shared `export function scopedCargoArgs(subcommand: "build" | "test" | "clippy", packages: string[], extraArgs?: string[]): string[]` emitting `["cargo", subcommand, ...packages.flatMap(p => ["-p", p]), ...(extraArgs ?? [])]`. Refactor `scopedCargoTestArgs(packages)` into a thin wrapper: `return scopedCargoArgs("test", packages, ["--quiet"])` â€” IDENTICAL output to today (non-empty â†’ `["cargo","test","-p",pâ€¦,"--quiet"]`; empty â†’ `["cargo","test","--quiet"]`), so `verify.ts`/`implementation.ts` callers and existing tests are unchanged. Add `export function scopedCargoBuildArgs(packages: string[]): string[]` â†’ `scopedCargoArgs("build", packages, ["--quiet"])` and `export function scopedCargoClippyArgs(packages: string[]): string[]` â†’ `scopedCargoArgs("clippy", packages, ["--all-targets", "--quiet"])`. Empty-set invariants: all three helpers return byte-identical workspace-wide argv (`["cargo","build","--quiet"]`, `["cargo","test","--quiet"]`, `["cargo","clippy","--all-targets","--quiet"]`) so the no-scoping path is unchanged.

**(3) runBuildGate auto-scoping of all three commands â€” AC-03.** Replace the current `testPackages` resolution block (lines ~286-301) with a FOUR-tier precedence resolver: (i) `opts.testPackages !== undefined` â†’ `dedupePreservingOrder(opts.testPackages)` (explicit `[]` forces workspace-wide, and the git-diff spawn is SKIPPED); (ii) else if `process.env.SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES` set â†’ `parseTestPackages(...)`; (iii) else if `cmds0.language === "rust"` â†’ `detectTouchedCargoPackages(cwd)`; (iv) else â†’ `[]` (workspace-wide). Note: git-diff MUST NOT run when tier (i) or (ii) supplies a value (no wasted spawn). Then, when `cmds0.language === "rust" && resolvedSet.length > 0`, build a shallow-copy override that replaces ALL THREE of `cmds.build`, `cmds.test`, `cmds.typecheck` (currently only `cmds.test` is replaced at :301): `cmds = { ...cmds0, build: scopedCargoBuildArgs(set), test: scopedCargoTestArgs(set), typecheck: scopedCargoClippyArgs(set) }`. When `set.length === 0` â†’ `cmds = cmds0` byte-identical to today (no override). `detectProjectCommands` stays PURE â€” scoping is applied only on the shallow copy, preserving the detector regression assertion. The three callers (`verify.ts:87`, `implementation.ts:64`, `stages/index.ts:53`) still pass only `{ signal }` â€” scoping is derived internally, call signatures unchanged.

**(4) In-scope classification â€” AC-04.** Extend `BuildGateResult` with two additive fields: `outOfScopeErrors: string[]` and `inScopePass: boolean`. After the `exec` loop collects `errors`, run a pure `function classifyOutOfScopeErrors(errors: string[], scopedSet: string[]): { inScopeErrors: string[]; outOfScopeErrors: string[] }`. Contract: for each error block, extract referenced crate names via (a) `--> <path>` / `--> crates/<pkg>/â€¦` markers using regex `crates\/([^/]+)\//`, and (b) cargo test-failure `-p <pkg>` markers using regex `/(?:^|\s)-p\s+(\S+)/`. An error is OUT-OF-SCOPE iff it references â‰¥1 crate AND EVERY referenced crate is NOT in `scopedSet`; otherwise (no marker found, or â‰¥1 referenced crate IS in scope, or `scopedSet` is empty) â†’ IN-SCOPE (conservative; ambiguity never grants false green). Compute `inScopePass = !pass && errors.length > 0 && outOfScopeErrors.length === errors.length` (ALL failures out-of-scope/pre-existing). When `pass === true`, set `inScopePass = true` and `outOfScopeErrors = []` (classification is a no-op â€” the gate already passed). The classifier MUST NEVER throw (try/catch the whole pass; on any error treat all as in-scope). When no scoping is active (`scopedSet` empty / non-rust), `outOfScopeErrors = []` and `inScopePass` stays false for any failure â†’ current abort semantics preserved exactly.

### Module: src/stages/implementation.ts

**(5) In-scope verdict in retry loop â€” AC-05.** After `const gate = runBuildGate(setup.worktreePath, { signal: ctx.signal })` (line 64, signature unchanged), compute the phase's GREEN condition as `gate.pass || gate.inScopePass`. When green via `inScopePass`, emit a distinct log line: ``ctx.log(`Implementation ${phaseId} IN-SCOPE GREEN on attempt ${attempt} â€” ${gate.outOfScopeErrors.length} pre-existing out-of-scope failure(s) ignored (crates: ${cratesFromErrors(gate.outOfScopeErrors).join(",")})`)``. Keep the existing PASS/FAIL log. The `if (!green) { â€¦ "failed after N attempts â€” terminating early"; allGreen=false; break; }` path fires ONLY when NEITHER `pass` nor `inScopePass` holds after `MAX_ATTEMPTS` (genuine in-scope failures). This stops the false-abort while still terminating on real in-scope breakage. (`cratesFromErrors` is a small local helper reusing the `crates/<pkg>/` + `-p <pkg>` extraction, or the out-of-scope crate list is precomputed and stored on the result to avoid re-parsing â€” implementation detail, prefer storing the resolved scoped crate set on the result for the log.)

### Data flow / invariant preservation
- `detectProjectCommands` purity: unchanged; all overrides applied on shallow copies only.
- Workspace-wide byte-identity: empty scoped set â‡’ argvs and result identical to pre-change (modulo two additive fields that never block).
- Process budget: +1 spawn only in the auto-touched tier (iii) for rust repos with no explicit/env override; all other tiers add zero spawns. No `shell:true` ever; package names are discrete argv elements.
- The three existing callers are call-site-compatible (still `{ signal }`); no signature change propagates.
control.testingStrategy:
TESTING STRATEGY. Primary oracle: deterministic unit + integration tests in `src/build-runner.test.ts` (new file) and a focused `implementation` stage test, executed by `npm test` (vitest run) + `npm run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit strict). No new runtime deps; `spawnSync` and `git` are stubbed/mocked so tests are hermetic and fast.

(a) `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (AC-01 â†’ SCENARIO-001/002/003/020/022/023): stub `spawnSync` to return synthetic `git diff --name-only` stdout; assert `crates/data/src/lib.rs` + `crates/api/src/main.rs` â†’ `["data","api"]` (order preserved, deduped); `crates/data/a.rs` + `crates/data/b.rs` â†’ `["data"]` (dedupe); duplicate `crates/data/â€¦` lines â†’ single entry; env `SUPER_DEV_GATE_BASE_REF=develop` flows into the `--merge-base develop` argv; non-crate-only diff (`Cargo.toml`, `README.md`) â†’ `[]`; empty diff / `r.status!==0` / thrown error â†’ `[]`; assert the helper NEVER throws (wrap a throwing stub, expect `[]`). Prefer injecting the spawn function or spying on `child_process.spawnSync` so the assertion reads the actual argv.

(b) Scoped argv family (AC-02 â†’ SCENARIO-004/005): assert `scopedCargoBuildArgs(["data","api"])` == `["cargo","build","-p","data","-p","api","--quiet"]`; `scopedCargoTestArgs(["data"])` == `["cargo","test","-p","data","--quiet"]` (unchanged); `scopedCargoClippyArgs(["data"])` == `["cargo","clippy","-p","data","--all-targets","--quiet"]`; empty-set â†’ byte-identical workspace-wide forms for all three; assert `scopedCargoTestArgs` still returns identical output to the pre-refactor implementation (regression of existing call sites).

(c) runBuildGate auto-scoping (AC-03 â†’ SCENARIO-006/007/008/017): integration test that stubs `spawnSync` (a) to return a touched-`data` git-diff on the first call family and (b) to capture/succeed the build/test/clippy spawns; in a temp `Cargo.toml` worktree with no env and no explicit packages, assert the captured build, test, AND typecheck argvs all carry `-p data`. Precedence tests: explicit `opts.testPackages=["api"]` â‡’ git-diff spawn NEVER happens and `-p api` used; `SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES="api"` set â‡’ `-p api` used; explicit `[]` â‡’ workspace-wide argvs and no git spawn.

(d) In-scope classification (AC-04 â†’ SCENARIO-009/010/011/021/024/028): unit-test the pure classifier with scoped set `{data}`: an error block referencing `crates/data/src/lib.rs` is IN-SCOPE; an error block referencing only `crates/compute/src/lib.rs` is OUT-OF-SCOPE; an error with NO parseable crate marker is IN-SCOPE (conservative); an error referencing both `data` and `compute` is IN-SCOPE (mixed). `inScopePass` true iff ALL errors out-of-scope and `!pass`; `inScopePass` true and `outOfScopeErrors=[]` when `pass`; empty scoped set â‡’ `outOfScopeErrors=[]`, `inScopePass=false` for any failure. Use realistic cargo error strings (`error[E0308]: â€¦ --> crates/compute/src/jobs.rs:42:10`, `failures: -p compute --test job_queries_test`).

(e) implementation.ts retry loop (AC-05 â†’ SCENARIO-012/013/014/025/027): test the stage with a stubbed `runBuildGate` returning `{pass:false, inScopePass:true, outOfScopeErrors:[â€¦computeâ€¦]}` on attempt 1 â†’ assert phase is GREEN, the IN-SCOPE GREEN log line is emitted, no early termination, and the commit proceeds; a result with `{pass:false, inScopePass:false}` for 3 attempts â†’ assert "terminating early" + `allGreen=false` + loop breaks; a `{pass:true}` result â†’ normal GREEN path.

(f) Backward-compat / non-regression (AC-06 â†’ SCENARIO-015/016/026): non-cargo repo (node/go/python manifests) â‡’ argvs identical, result identical modulo two additive fields; non-git / no-touched-crates / unset env â‡’ workspace-wide argvs; zero failures â‡’ `inScopePass` never blocks.

Gate: `npm run typecheck` must be clean (strict); `npm test` must pass for existing AND new suites. No new runtime dependencies; no network; no real `git`/`cargo` execution in CI (all spawned).

findings.title: Hallucinated git flag `--merge-only` in the spec Scope sentence
findings.detail: 06-specification.md line 13 (Scope) says the change adds "one `git diff --merge-only`". `git diff --merge-only` is NOT a valid git option. Every authoritative source â€” the spec's own Architecture Â§1, Testing (a), AC-01/AC-07 in 01-requirements.md, the BDD doc, debug-analysis, research-report, plan, and task-list â€” uses the correct `git diff --merge-base <baseRef> --name-only`. An implementer who copies the Scope sentence literally would spawn a failing command; because detectTouchedCargoPackages wraps the spawn in try/catchâ†’[], the failure would silently degrade to workspace-wide scoping, invisibly defeating the entire auto-scope capability (AC-01/AC-03) with no test catching it. Recommend correcting the Scope sentence to `git diff --merge-base <baseRef> --name-only`.

findings.title: `opts.packages` referenced in precedence but never defined
findings.detail: The precedence clause (06-specification.md line 21, mirrored from AC-03 in 01-requirements.md:19) lists "explicit `opts.testPackages`/`opts.packages`". But `opts.packages` is never defined: the current runBuildGate opts type only has `testPackages`, Architecture Â§3 tier (i) resolves ONLY `opts.testPackages !== undefined`, and all three call sites pass `{signal}` only. It is therefore ambiguous whether `opts.packages` is a new opt field to add or vestigial text. Since the binding architecture tier never reads it, an implementer adding it would be gold-plating, while an implementer ignoring it would still satisfy the architecture. Recommend dropping `opts.packages` from the precedence clause, or â€” if a second explicit channel is genuinely wanted â€” adding it to the opts type and tier (i) resolution.

findings.title: NFR requires a timeout envelope the implementation spec omits
findings.detail: NFR (01-requirements.md:27) states the git-diff helper "must use the existing spawnSync pattern with the resolved timeout envelope, never an unbounded shell." But Architecture Â§1 (06-specification.md) specifies the spawn as `spawnSync("git", [...], { encoding: "utf8" })` with NO timeout, unlike the existing exec closure which passes `{ cwd, timeout: timeoutMs, encoding: "utf8" }`. A `git diff --name-only` is fast in practice, but the spec contradicts its own NFR. Recommend either adding a bounded `timeout` (e.g. a small fixed cap or the resolved timeoutMs) to the detectTouchedCargoPackages spawnSync, or explicitly NFR-exempting this fast spawn and reconciling the two statements.

findings.title: `inScopePass` formula contradicts its own prose
findings.detail: AC-04 (01-requirements.md:20) and Architecture Â§4 (06-specification.md) both define `inScopePass = !pass && errors.length>0 && outOfScopeErrors.length===errors.length` (which is FALSE when pass===true), yet in the same breath state "When `pass` is true, `inScopePass` is true and classification is a no-op." The two rules are mutually exclusive and an implementer must pick one. It is operationally harmless because the consumer is `gate.pass || gate.inScopePass` (short-circuits when pass), but the contradiction invites divergent implementations and a conflicting unit test. Recommend stating that `inScopePass` is a don't-care/irrelevant when `pass` is true, or providing a single consistent rule.

findings.title: Two non-identical crate-extraction regexes and a dual-option log helper
findings.detail: Architecture Â§1 extracts crates via `/(?:^|\/)crates\/([^/]+)\//` (left-anchored at start or slash), while Â§4's classifier uses `crates\/([^/]+)\/` (no left anchor) and a separate `-p <pkg>` regex `/(?:^|\s)-p\s+(\S+)/`. The unanchored Â§4 variant can match differently on paths like `foo/crates/x/`. Additionally Â§5 offers two interchangeable strategies for the IN-SCOPE GREEN log (a local `cratesFromErrors` helper vs storing the resolved set on the result). Neither blocks implementation, but two near-duplicate regexes risk inconsistent classification between detection and logging. Recommend canonicalizing one crate-extraction regex used by both the detector and the classifier, and picking one log-helper strategy.

Security is solid: no `shell:true` anywhere, `baseRef`/package names flow as discrete argv elements (no injection surface), no secrets in logs. One Medium defect found: the IN-SCOPE GREEN log extractor in implementation.ts re-scans the FULL error block (label + tail) for `-p <pkg>` markers and so re-admits the scoped command label's own `-p <pkg>`, producing a misleading "ignored out-of-scope crates: data,compute" log that lists the IN-SCOPE crate as ignored. This is observability-only (the gate verdict itself is correct because the classifier in build-runner.ts properly excludes the label via flagRegion slicing), but it directly contradicts the feature's semantic intent and is untested because the stage-test fixture omits the real label+tail block shape. findings.title: IN-SCOPE GREEN log lists the in-scope crate among "ignored out-of-scope crates" (test/prod gap)
findings.detail: `cratesFromErrors(gate.outOfScopeErrors)` re-scans the WHOLE error block with `pkgRe = /(?:^|\s)-p\s+(\S+)/g` to populate the `(crates: â€¦)` portion of the IN-SCOPE GREEN log. But `runBuildGate` assembles each error block as `${label} FAILED (${reason}):\n${tail}` where the label is e.g. `cargo build -p data --quiet` â€” i.e. the block STRING still carries the scoped command's own `-p data`. So for a real out-of-scope failure the extractor collects BOTH `data` (from the label) and `compute` (from the tail), and the log prints `pre-existing out-of-scope failure(s) ignored (crates: data,compute)`. That lists `data` â€” the active in-scope crate â€” as an ignored pre-existing crate, which is exactly the distinction the whole feature exists to make and is the operator's only window into why a phase committed despite failures. The gate VERDICT is unaffected because `classifyOutOfScopeErrors` in build-runner.ts carefully excludes the label by scanning `-p` only on the post-` FAILED (` flagRegion; `cratesFromErrors` does NOT replicate that exclusion, so the two are inconsistent. The bug is untested: the stage-test fixture `GATE_INSCOPE_PASS` uses a bare `"error[E0308]: ... --> crates/compute/..."` string with no label, so `cratesFromErrors` happens to yield only `compute` and the assertion `toContain("compute")` passes â€” but a fixture matching the real label+tail shape (e.g. `"cargo build -p data --quiet FAILED (exit 101):\n  --> crates/compute/src/jobs.rs:42:10"`) would expose `data` in the output. Suggested fix: in `cratesFromErrors`, apply the same `indexOf(" FAILED (")` flagRegion slice before running `pkgRe` (scan pathRe on the whole block, scan pkgRe only on the tail), or â€” better â€” export a single `referencedCrates(block)` helper from build-runner.ts and reuse it in both the classifier and the log extractor to eliminate the duplicated regex logic that caused the divergence. Add a stage test whose fixture carries the real label+tail shape to pin the contract.

findings.title: Duplicated crate-extraction regex across build-runner.ts and implementation.ts (DRY)
findings.detail: `cratesFromErrors` duplicates `pathRe = /crates\/([^/]+)\//g` and a `-p` extractor that already live (with subtly different, label-aware semantics) in `classifyOutOfScopeErrors`. This duplication is the root cause of F-01: the two drifted (the classifier gained flagRegion slicing; the log extractor did not). Consolidating into one shared, exported helper would prevent future drift and shrink the surface. Non-blocking; mention as cleanup.

findings.title: git-diff spawn is not AbortSignal/timeout-aware
findings.detail: `detectTouchedCargoPackages` calls `spawnSync("git", [...], { encoding: "utf8" })` with no `timeout` and does not consult `opts.signal` (the helper signature doesn't take opts). `runBuildGate`'s other spawns honor both `resolveTimeoutMs()` and `opts.signal?.aborted`. In practice `git diff --merge-base <ref> --name-only` is fast and bounded so this is a negligible real-world risk, and the spec explicitly permits exactly this one extra spawn. Noting only for completeness: if a base ref triggers an expensive merge-base walk on a huge repo, or git hangs, the gate would block longer than the configured per-command timeout. Acceptable as-is; consider passing a conservative timeout (e.g. a few seconds) in a future pass.

control.title: Adversarial Review â€” spec-03-scope-aware-build-gate
control.summary:
The implementation is well-engineered at the unit level: typecheck is clean (tsc --noEmit), all 674 tests pass, the change is backward-compatible (empty-set â‡’ byte-identical workspace-wide argv; additive BuildGateResult fields; classifier never throws and never grants a false green), and the scoped-argv family + precedence + auto-detection + classifier are each independently testable with good coverage. The four-tier precedence and the "explicit [] forces workspace-wide" semantics are correct.

However, the work only partially achieves its stated intent ("stop false-failing and false-aborting on messy real-world monorepos") for three structural reasons that standard review would miss:

(1) ASYMMETRY â€” the in-scope verdict is wired into exactly ONE of three gate consumers. implementation.ts (Stage 9.2) honors gate.inScopePass, but verify.ts buildGate (Stage 9) returns only {pass, ran, errors} and the Stage 10 reviewFix step feeds the FULL errors array to the implementer with "make these pass." So the same pre-existing out-of-scope clippy/test failures that no longer abort implementation will re-surface in verify's gate and, worse, will actively induce the implementer to edit out-of-scope crates during reviewFix â€” the literal opposite of scope-awareness. The false-abort relocates rather than disappears.

(2) CLASSIFIER FRAGILITY â€” out-of-scope detection depends on a `crates/<pkg>/` or `-p <pkg>` marker surviving into the captured 12-line stderr/stdout tail. Test-failure listings (`failures:`, `---- name stdout ----`) and final summaries frequently contain neither marker, so the commonest pre-existing-failure type (a failing test in an untouched crate) is often classified IN-SCOPE and the false-abort persists. It is conservative (no false-green) but defeats the feature for its primary use case.

(3) LAYOUT/REF ASSUMPTIONS â€” auto-detection only matches a `crates/<pkg>/` directory convention and defaults the base ref to `main`; repos using top-level member dirs, `members=["*"]`, or branches like `master`/`trunk` silently fall back to workspace-wide with no signal, in exactly the failure mode this PR was built to fix.

None of these are production-failure / data-loss / security risks, so the calibrated verdict is CONTEST (quality concerns requiring author response), not REJECT. Recommend: thread inScopePass into verify.ts + filter reviewFix's buildErrors to inScopeErrors; widen marker capture (larger tail or parse full cargo output, incl. the `rerun \`cargo test -p <pkg>\`` note); detect the real default branch or emit a one-line log when the base ref is unresolved.

findings.title: In-scope verdict applied in only 1 of 3 gate consumers â€” false-abort relocates to verify/reviewFix
findings.detail: gate.inScopePass / outOfScopeErrors are consumed ONLY in src/stages/implementation.ts:85-92. verify.ts buildGateStep (verify.ts:81-90) returns `{ pass: r.pass, ran, errors }` â€” it drops inScopePass entirely â€” and `buildGreen` (verify.ts:42-45) keys off `b.pass !== false`. The Stage 10 reviewFix step (verify.ts:101-110) then feeds the FULL `s.buildGate.errors` into the implementer prompt as '## Build/test gate failures (make these pass)'. Net effect: the pre-existing out-of-scope failures that implementation.ts now treats as GREEN still (a) make verify's gate FAIL, and (b) are handed verbatim to the implementer during reviewFix as things to fix â€” actively inducing edits to out-of-scope crates (scope creep), the opposite of the feature's goal. The change is scoped to two files per the task constraints, but the consequence is that the root cause (blunt gate) is only half-addressed; the abort moves from Stage 9.2 to Stage 9/10. Recommend threading inScopePass into verify.ts's gate result and filtering reviewFix's buildErrors to the in-scope subset (gate has outOfScopeErrors available â€” surface it).

findings.title: Out-of-scope classification hinges on crate markers surviving a 12-line tail â€” fragile for the headline use case
findings.detail:
classifyOutOfScopeErrors (build-runner.ts) extracts `crates/<pkg>/` and `-p <pkg>` markers. But the gate only retains the LAST 12 lines: `const tail = (r.stderr||r.stdout||'').trim().split('
').slice(-STDERR_TAIL_LINES)...` (STDERR_TAIL_LINES=12). A pre-existing failing TEST in an out-of-scope crate commonly produces a tail like `running N tests
test foo::bar ... FAILED
...
failures:
---- foo::bar stdout ----` with NEITHER a `crates/` path NOR a `-p` marker in those last 12 lines (the panic-location line `crates/compute/tests/x.rs:NN` and cargo's `rerun \`cargo test -p compute\`` note sit earlier in the output). Such a block is classified IN-SCOPE (conservative default), so inScopePass stays false and the false-abort persists â€” for precisely the failure class the PR targets (reports_test.rs, job_queries_test.rs in the evidence). The classifier is sound for compile/clippy errors (which always emit `--> crates/<pkg>/...`) but unreliable for test failures. Recommend enlarging the retained tail for rust, or scanning full stdout for the rerun `-p <pkg>` note, before relying on inScopePass.

findings.title: Dependency-cascade can commit in-scope code that did not actually build
findings.detail: When the in-scope crate (e.g. `data`) depends on an out-of-scope crate (`compute`) that has a pre-existing compile error, `cargo build -p data` transitively builds `compute`, which fails with `--> crates/compute/...` markers. The classifier marks that error OUT-OF-SCOPE (compute âˆ‰ touched set) â‡’ inScopePass=true â‡’ implementation.ts treats the phase GREEN and commits. But `data` itself never compiled successfully â€” its dependency broke. The classifier cannot distinguish 'compute failed independently' from 'compute failed as a transitive dep of the in-scope build.' This can let genuinely broken in-scope code ship. Mitigation is non-trivial (would need the #3 baseline-diff explicitly deferred), so at minimum the summary/log should flag when out-of-scope crates appear in the build graph of an in-scope crate.

findings.title: Documented env example produces a broken cargo argv
findings.detail: The module docstring (build-runner.ts, SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES section) gives `Example: SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES="crates/api,crates/store"`. But parseTestPackages only trims/dedupes â€” it does NOT strip a `crates/` prefix â€” so this value flows straight into `cargo test -p crates/api --quiet`, which cargo rejects (invalid package spec; `-p` wants a name/glob, not a path). A user following the documented example gets a hard gate failure. Either fix the doc example to `api,store` or have parseTestPackages strip an optional `crates/` prefix. (The classifier is unaffected because pathRe requires a trailing slash and `-p crates/api` has none, so no false-green â€” but the gate itself breaks.)

findings.title: Auto-detection only matches `crates/<pkg>/`; other Cargo layouts and a wrong base ref silently no-op
findings.detail: detectTouchedCargoPackages regex is `/(?:^|\/)crates\/([^/]+)\//` â€” it hardcodes the `crates/<pkg>/` convention. Workspaces with top-level member dirs (`members = ["api","data"]`), `members=["*"]`, or nested layouts produce [] â‡’ workspace-wide â‡’ the feature silently does nothing. Separately, the base ref defaults to `main` and a non-zero git exit (repo on `master`/`trunk`/`develop`, or only `origin/main` present) also yields [] with NO log signal â€” so the headline benefit vanishes in the exact failure mode (false-abort on a repo whose default branch isn't `main`) this PR was meant to fix. Recommend: (a) fall back to `git symbolic-ref refs/remotes/origin/HEAD` / `git config init.defaultBranch` when `main` is unresolved, and (b) emit one `ctx.log`/console line when auto-detection resolves to [] so operators can tell scoping is inactive.

findings.title: Duplicated, divergent marker-extraction logic between classifier and implementation.ts
findings.detail: implementation.ts:22 cratesFromErrors re-implements the same two marker regexes as classifyOutOfScopeErrors but DIVERGES: it scans the WHOLE error block (including the `cargo test -p data ... FAILED` label, which always carries the scoped packages) and uses `(?:^|\s)-p` instead of the classifier's `(?<!\w)-p`. Consequence: the 'crates: ...' suffix in the IN-SCOPE GREEN log will always echo the in-scope scoped packages from the label, not the actual out-of-scope failure crates â€” misleading. Two implementations of one contract is a drift hazard. Extract a single shared helper (or have runBuildGate return the resolved failure-crate list) and reuse it.

findings.title: git diff re-spawned on every gate call; touched set is invariant within a run
findings.detail: implementation.ts calls runBuildGate up to 3Ã— per phase Ã— N phases; each call, when no higher precedence tier applies, re-runs detectTouchedCargoPackages â†’ `git -C <cwd> diff --merge-base main --name-only`. The touched-crate set cannot change during a single pipeline run, so this is up to ~3Ã—N redundant process spawns (plus their git I/O on large diffs). Compute once (e.g. resolve in the stage and pass via opts.testPackages, or memoize inside the module keyed by cwd+ref) and reuse. Not a correctness issue â€” pure waste.

findings.title: scopedCargoArgs exported for only two internal call sites
findings.detail: scopedCargoArgs is exported (public module surface) yet is consumed only by scopedCargoBuildArgs/scopedCargoTestArgs/scopedCargoClippyArgs inside the same file. No external caller needs it. Could be unexported (module-private) to keep the public API minimal and prevent accidental dependence on the raw core. Minor; the type-narrowing CargoSubcommand union is a nice touch.
